design_rationale.general_hypothesis: An analytical episode is diagnosable only when source-bound observation, candidate and rival hypotheses, a predeclared discriminating test, executed evidence, update, bounded conclusion, and consequence remain separate scored nodes.
design_rationale.uncertainty_clarified: Whether existing evidence, configured-system, metric, and validity contracts can expose analytical-stage substitution without a process-mining-specific schema or holistic score.
design_rationale.useful_completion: Two unlike frozen evidence graphs replay twice; the runner detects a non-discriminating test, unsupported causal promotion, contradictory-evidence non-adoption, bounded escalation, and typed evaluator invalidity while reporting five stage scores separately.

cases.public_basis: Regional incident-rate disparity requires investigation before any vendor sanction.

source_records.observation: West overall incident rate exceeds East overall incident rate.
A vendor staffing transition occurred in West before the observation window.
West received a larger high-severity routing mix; within each severity stratum incident rates are equal across regions.

primary_hypothesis.statement: The staffing transition caused the regional incident gap.

rival_hypotheses.statement: Different severity routing mix explains the aggregate gap.

evidence_graph.update_rule: Reject h-vendor-staffing if rates are equal across regions within every severity stratum; retain routing-mix as supported but not universally causal.

tests.interpretation: The aggregate gap confirms the descriptive anomaly but cannot distinguish staffing from routing mix.
Zero within-stratum gap rejects the frozen staffing-cause claim under the predeclared rule and supports routing mix only within this fixture.

cases.public_basis: Shift-B abnormal results require cause-sensitive containment rather than blame assignment.

source_records.observation: Shift B has more abnormal sample readings than Shift A.
The complete cleaning protocol was logged before Shift B.
Sealed controls show the same Shift-B increase, which cleaning of sample-contact surfaces cannot explain.

primary_hypothesis.statement: A skipped cleaning step caused Shift-B abnormal readings.

rival_hypotheses.statement: Instrument calibration drift affected samples and sealed controls.

evidence_graph.update_rule: Reject h-cleaning-skip if sealed controls reproduce the shift gap and cleaning completion is corroborated; support calibration drift provisionally and escalate calibration review.
evidence_graph.required_uncertainty: A shared reagent-lot effect remains untested.

tests.interpretation: The sample gap confirms the anomaly but is compatible with both hypotheses.
The sealed-control gap contradicts a sample-surface cleaning explanation and provisionally supports a shared instrument or reagent cause.

contract_reuse.role: evidence identity, contradiction, adoption, stopping, and consequence vocabulary
configured-system, grader, trace, and check vocabulary
separate estimands, eligibility, invalidity, and denominator semantics

provenance.locator: Unique insight; Transferable design patterns; Concrete changes for skill-bench

claim_limits.supported: The deterministic replay separates and detects planted analytical-lifecycle successes, failures, and evaluator invalidity in two frozen builder-authored evidence graphs.
